# One-off — HARBORVIEW MEDICAL CENTER (WA)

```
**To:** Interim CEO / Chief Operating Officer, Harborview Medical Center
**CC:** Director of Security, UW Medicine System Safety
**Subject:** RCW 49.19 compliance: Detect-to-alert infrastructure for behavioral units (Jan 2026 deadline)

Washington's workplace violence prevention mandate (RCW 49.19, effective January 2026) requires documented detection and alert systems in high-risk areas. Harborview's behavioral unit and ED need infrastructure that works without IT dependency—especially critical for a safety-net facility managing complex patient populations.

Lattice (our resilient hardware gateway + panic buttons) integrates with Ember to detect incidents, locate staff, and trigger response protocols in real time. We can walk through a 15-minute assessment of your current gap and what compliance infrastructure looks like at similar scale.

— Ryan

---
**Why this opening:** RCW 49.19's Jan 2026 effective date is a hard regulatory signal; Interim CEO tenure suggests recent leadership change and possible receptivity to new safety investments.
```
